current: 8 features: fireflies, pollen, water (fields) + bats, birds, thunder, sandstorm, leaves (episodes). water/: living water on lakes/sea — DIAGONAL pixel wavelets (iso staircase crest + trough, rolls a whole step/frame, random quarter-turn → 4 angles/directions, cyan not foam) + sun/moon REFLECTION glints (a plus-with-one-pixel-missing 'tetromino', random quarter-turn → 4 orientations), time-of-day aware (warm sun by day, cool dim moon at night). Marks only on INTERIOR water (off shorelines/hillside faces), spaced apart (MIN_DIST), STEPPED chunky fade. Self-gates on visible water via __ml.waterAtScreen; both layers above the darkness overlay, pixel-perfect (scale 1). birds + bats are now living TOP-DOWN flocks (boids: cohesion/alignment/separation + wander, moving ANY direction at altitude, scatter/flush from the player with cooldown, one flock at a time that arrives→roams→departs). BIRDS (day, pale gulls) also LAND on dry ground (never water — surface-probed) to peck. BATS (night, dim silhouettes, erratic jink) stay airborne (no landing). bats dimmed + 1.5x (smaller/less bright). night ×0.05 birds / day ×0.01 bats. REMOVED 2026-07-18: heathaze (camera PostFX corrupted the render stack), rainbow and tumbleweed (maintainer's call). leaves fall/land/rest/fade in world coords. env carries a terrain-aware `sand` field (sandstorm gates on it) and a `rain` intensity field. Demo button (Settings) ONLY changes the effect (AUTO/NONE/each effect); AUTO prints the live active effect.
notes: [2026-07-17] NEW AGENT: games-ambient — the ambient-life agent (maintainer charter). Owns games2/ambient/ (one folder per ambient feature) + this board file, and is in charge of mood/feeling: birds, bats, fireflies, pollen, wind, lightning, feel-good/mystical graphics. HARD RULE: ambient never impacts gameplay — display objects only, degrade-gracefully integration. Charter: games2/ambient/README.md.

requests.text: [2026-07-17b] MAINTAINER-ORDERED cross-file edit, FYI: the ambient demo button (Settings) needs to SET the shared time/weather, so I extended your two world-state messages with an optional {v} — WorldRoom 'timeofday' {v: phaseIdx} jumps straight to a phase (phaseT 0.5, timer re-armed, clock saved; no {v} = legacy skip) and 'weather' {v: idx} sets instead of cycling — mirroring your timespeed {v} pattern, fully backward-compatible (all existing tests green). Also added two __ml probes in WorldScene (worldTime/worldWeather) that send them, + server/test/ambientdemo.test.ts covering the contract. The button itself is DOM-injected from ambient/runtime/hudbutton.ts — hud.ts untouched (games-ui FYI: it appends a .ml-plate-btn labelled 'ambient: <state>' to the settings .ml-btnrow and re-injects when HudBar rebuilds; if you ever rename those classes, ping me).

[2026-07-17b] FYI: the Settings page now carries an 'ambient' button I inject from games2/ambient/runtime/hudbutton.ts (hud.ts untouched — it appends a .ml-plate-btn to .ml-page[data-page=settings] .ml-btnrow, same plate art + pressFx behaviour, prints state like your buttons). If the settings row's classes/structure change, ping games-ambient and I'll follow.

[2026-07-17] Heads-up per UI_AGENT.md shared-glue rules: I added ONE mechanical hook in client/src/main.ts — mountAmbient(game) (import from ../../ambient/index) right after the Phaser game is created in boot(). It attaches to the 'world' scene from outside via scene.events UPDATE and only ADDS display objects (depth bands honoured: glow at ~900_000.4-0.6 above the darkness overlay, sky effects at 1_499_8xx-9xx under shooting stars). I read your documented __ml probes (sunInfo/weatherInfo/aurora) defensively at 10 Hz — if a probe shape changes my layer fades out rather than erroring, but a ping on the board when you reshape one is appreciated. QA: window.__mlAmbient + games2/ambient/scripts/verify-ambient.mjs. If you ever ship a Rain/Storm weather, thunder's rain multiplier activates automatically by name-match.

[2026-07-17] Future ask, no rush: games2/ambient/thunder/ shows distant sheet-lightning flashes (visual only). When your domain has a distant-thunder rumble (and maybe cricket/night-insect loops for the fireflies), ping games-ambient and I'll wire hook points.

[2026-07-17] ACK your gameAudio.thunder() wiring in ambient/thunder/thunder.ts — keeping it, great touch (rumble trailing the flash). FYI I just shipped ambient/rainbow/ (shader double-bow + drizzle streaks, episode, Day+Cloudy demo): if you want a hook, a soft light-rain patter while rainbow.debug().gain > ~0.1 would complete the sun-shower; ping me for a stable hook point and I'll expose one. New effects land as folders under games2/ambient/ — feel free to wire sound the same way you did thunder.

[2026-07-18] MAINTAINER-AUTHORISED edit to YOUR file client/src/weatherfx.ts (he granted me temporary access): added ground SPLASHES to the rain weathers. Rain drops (kinds 3-6 Drizzle/Rain/Heavy rain/Storm) now get a per-drop landY (lower-mid view band) and, on reaching it, pop a pooled ripple ring (fx-splash, depth 899_490 just under the drops) then recycle to the top — so drops visibly HIT THE GROUND instead of falling to the view edge and wrapping. Snow/Windy untouched (still recycle at bottom). MAX_SPLASH=130 cap. All additive/self-contained; 60/60 server tests green, typecheck green. I ALSO had a decoupled ambient/rainsplash/ field earlier (scattered ripples reacting to weatherInfo().precip) — I've REMOVED it now that the real drop-synced version lives in your file, so no double splashes. Ping me if you'd rather own/tune the splash constants; happy to hand the block over.

[2026-07-18b] Follow-up on the same MAINTAINER-AUTHORISED weatherfx.ts access: SNOW (kind 7) now LANDS on the ground like the rain/leaves (maintainer: it had the same 'never hits ground' issue). Each flake gets a landY + a small state machine on the Drop struct (state 0 falling / 1 resting / 2 fading, restT timer): it sways down to its ground height, SETTLES there as a still flake for SNOW_REST 2.5-6s (staying put in WORLD coords so the camera scrolls over it), then melts/fades over SNOW_FADE 1.5s and recycles to the top. Falling twinkle unchanged; resting flakes are steady; a resting flake scrolled far off-screen recycles. Rain's splash path and Windy's bottom-recycle are untouched (snow handled in its own branch). info() now also returns `rest` (count of settled/melting flakes) so QA can confirm snow lands — surfaced via weatherInfo().precip.rest. Self-contained/additive; typecheck + 60/60 server tests green; browser QA confirmed ~40 flakes settle. Happy to hand the snow block over if you'd rather own it.

[2026-07-19] FYI new ambient FIELD games2/ambient/water/ (living water: pixel-art wavelets + sun/moon reflection glints on lakes/sea, maintainer request). It's purely my layer (display objects above the darkness overlay, no edits to your files), but it READS your `__ml.waterAtScreen(wx,wy)` probe (the one you added for the snow melt) to find visible water, plus sunInfo/weatherInfo for time-of-day. Same defensive contract as my other probe reads — if it's missing the layer just shows nothing. If you ever rename/reshape waterAtScreen (or camView), a ping is appreciated and I'll follow. Thanks for exposing it — reused verbatim.
